message:
fix(fr-runtime): ACL SAVE/LOAD emits upstream 'not configured' error (partial br-frankenredis-faqe)

Upstream acl.c::aclCommand rejects SAVE/LOAD with
    "This Redis instance is not configured to use an ACL file.
     You may want to specify users via the ACL SETUSER command
     and then issue a CONFIG REWRITE (assuming you have a
     Redis configuration file set) in order to store users in
     the Redis configuration."
when `server.acl_filename[0] == '\0'`. Our runtime had its own
shorter "There is no configured ACL file" message and the
fixture harness auto-provisioned a dummy path, so both the
fixture test and the live oracle diverged.

- fr-runtime: new `ACL_FILE_NOT_CONFIGURED_ERR` const with the
  exact upstream wording; wire SAVE + LOAD + matching unit test
- configure_runtime_for_fixture: stop auto-setting acl_file_path
  for core_acl.json so our SAVE/LOAD return the proper error
  (upstream oracle runs without --aclfile too)
- core_acl.json: flip 5 SAVE/LOAD/roundtrip cases to expect the
  upstream error, and GETUSER-after-failed-LOAD to expect nil
- remove the 5 SAVE/LOAD XFAILs from the live-oracle ACL test

fr-runtime 275 passed; fr-command 639 passed;
fr-conformance 180 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
